title: Applying for a Part-Time Café Job

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: Hello, Cornerstone Café, Laura speaking.

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: Hi, I saw your advert for a part-time weekend job in the window, and I'd like to apply, please.

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: Great, thanks for calling. Let me take a few details and set up an interview. Could I have your full name?

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: Yes, it's Chloe Bennett.

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: And how do you spell the surname?

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: It's B-E-N-N-E-T-T. Bennett. Two Ns, two Ts.

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: Perfect, thank you. And a phone number I can reach you on?

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: It's oh-seven-five-three-three, four-one-nine, seven-eight-two.

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: Let me read that back — oh-seven-five-three-three, four-one-nine, seven-eight-two. Right?

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: That's it.

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: Now, have you done any similar work before?

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: Yes, I worked in a bakery for about a year, mostly serving customers and using the till. So I've got some experience with the coffee machine too.

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: That's really useful. The job is Saturdays and Sundays, from eight in the morning until two in the afternoon. The pay is eleven pounds fifty an hour, and you'd get a free lunch on shift.

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: That sounds great. I can definitely do both weekend days.

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: Wonderful. Now, we'd need you to start fairly soon. When could you begin?

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: I could start straight away — well, actually, I'm away next week, so the following weekend would be better. That would be the fourteenth.

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: The fourteenth is fine. Let's get you in for an interview first, though. Could you come in this Thursday at half past four?

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: Thursday at half past four, yes, that works.

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: Lovely. When you come, please bring a form of ID and, if you can, the name of someone who can give you a reference — a previous manager, ideally, not a family member.

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: No problem, I'll bring my passport and my old manager's details.

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: Perfect. Just so you can find us, we're the café on the corner of King Street, right opposite the post office. See you Thursday, Chloe.

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: Great, I know exactly where you mean. Is there anything I should know about the uniform, or what to wear?

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: We'll give you a branded apron and a T-shirt on your first day, so you don't need to buy anything. Just wear comfortable dark trousers and non-slip shoes, as the floor can get a bit wet behind the counter. And we ask staff to tie long hair back for hygiene.

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: That's all fine. Will the interview involve any kind of trial, or is it just a chat?

turns.speaker: Laura
turns.text: It's mostly a chat, but I might ask you to make a couple of coffees on the machine, just so I can see how you get on. Nothing to worry about — I'll talk you through it. It usually takes about twenty minutes altogether.

turns.speaker: Chloe
turns.text: Perfect, that sounds fine. Brilliant. Thank you so much, Laura. See you Thursday.

Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questionGroups.prompt: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questionGroups.instructions_html: Who does Laura suggest Chloe ask for a reference?

questions.prompt_text: For a reference, Chloe should name
